pages.metaDescription: Discover how Sisal built a responsible gaming community with social.plus, reaching 50,000 monthly active users within their first month of launch.
pages.content:
# How Sisal built a responsible gaming community with social.plus
Sisal is one of Italy's most established names in betting and gaming, with a long history of connecting players to the experiences they love while championing responsible gaming as a core part of their mission. Operating across a wide portfolio of products, Sisal has always understood that sustainable growth in the gaming industry isn't just about acquisition — it's about building an environment where players feel safe, informed, and genuinely engaged over the long term.
That belief has increasingly shaped how Sisal thinks about their digital product. As the platform grew and player expectations evolved, the team recognized that the next step wasn't just improving the betting experience itself. It was building the community layer around it.
<sprscript-green>The Challenge<sprscript-green>
## Creating a safer gaming experience through connection
Sisal set out to tackle one of the more nuanced challenges in the gaming industry: how do you build a community that actively promotes responsible gambling, rather than one that simply amplifies betting activity? The goal wasn't engagement for its own sake. It was to create a social environment where players could connect, share experiences, and make decisions with the benefit of peer context and collective knowledge.
Without that social layer, the platform experience was largely solitary. Players placed bets independently, with little visibility into how others were approaching the same markets or what the broader community was thinking. That isolation doesn't just limit engagement — it can also work against responsible gaming, since players lack the social signals and shared norms that naturally emerge in community environments.
Sisal needed infrastructure that could support genuine community interaction at scale, embed naturally within their existing platform, and be shaped to reflect their values around healthy and responsible gaming. Building that from scratch would have been a significant undertaking, both in initial development and in the sustained engineering effort required to maintain and evolve it.</p>
<sprscript-green>Why social.plus<sprscript-green>
## Social touchpoints designed for a "people playing together" experience
Sisal's vision was specific: social features that made players feel like they were part of a shared experience, not just parallel individuals using the same platform. That meant introducing touchpoints where users could see how others engaged, react to content, join groups around shared interests, and participate in conversations that added context and confidence to their own decisions.
social.plus offered the in-app community infrastructure to make that vision real without requiring Sisal to divert significant engineering resources toward building and maintaining social systems from the ground up. The modular approach meant Sisal could implement the features most relevant to their responsible gaming goals first, then expand as the community matured. Equally important, the community would live entirely within Sisal's own environment, keeping the experience cohesive and ensuring the platform retained full ownership of how the community was shaped and governed.
<sprscript-green>Implementation<sprscript-green>
## The building blocks of a connected gaming community
Sisal implemented a core set of social features designed to introduce meaningful interaction without overwhelming the existing product experience. Feeds gave players a shared space to follow activity and content across the community. Groups allowed users to gather around specific interests, teams, or betting markets, creating smaller and more focused communities within the broader platform. Comments and reactions added a lightweight but effective layer of real-time engagement, letting players respond to content and to each other in ways that feel natural and low-friction.
Together, these features created the foundation for a community experience where players could engage on their own terms — participating as much or as little as they chose, while always having access to the social context that makes the platform feel alive.
<sprscript-green>The Results<sprscript-green>
## 50,000 monthly active users in the first month
The response from Sisal's player base was immediate. Within the first month of launch, the platform reached its initial MAU threshold, achieving 50,000 monthly active users engaging with the new community features. For a community that had only just launched, that level of adoption validated the core premise: Sisal's players were ready for this experience, and the social layer resonated from day one.
The early results reflect something deeper than feature uptake. They suggest that when players are given a well-designed, purposeful space to connect, the engagement follows naturally. The "people playing together" vision Sisal set out to build has translated into a platform where community and responsible gaming reinforce each other, rather than pulling in opposite directions.
Sisal is now focused on building further on this foundation. Plans include enriching the platform with additional social features including stories and charts, which will deepen the ways players can share experiences and make sense of activity across the community. The long-term goal is a gaming environment where social connection and responsible play are woven into every part of the experience.

pages.metaDescription: Discover how The 1916 Company transformed their app into a collector community with social.plus — driving daily engagement through the Wrist Feed, discussions, and expert-led content.
pages.content:
# How The 1916 Company turned a transactional app into a collector community with social.plus
The 1916 Company is one of the most trusted names in luxury watches, jewelry, and handbags, built on the belief that collecting is about far more than ownership. Named after the year the founder's family began a legacy in fine timepieces, the company has grown into a leading destination for pre-owned and certified luxury goods, serving collectors across the United States and beyond. For their most engaged customers, buying a watch is just the beginning of a much deeper relationship with horology.
That relationship has always been at the heart of how The 1916 Company thinks about their business. Serious collectors don't just want to transact; they want to share, discuss, learn, and connect with others who understand the passion. For years, those connections happened through in-person events, expert curation, and white-glove service. But as collector behavior shifted and digital became the primary touchpoint, a new kind of platform was needed.
<sprscript-green>The Challenge<sprscript-green>
## A transaction-first app in a community-first world
The 1916 Company's app worked well as a commerce tool. Collectors could browse inventory, track pieces, and make purchases. But the experience ended at the point of sale. There was no space to share what was on your wrist, no way to discuss a major auction result with fellow collectors, and no place to engage with the editorial and expert content that defines the collector conversation.
Community building had historically relied on physical events, moments that created real connection but were inherently limited in reach and frequency. Between those events, there was no continuous digital touchpoint keeping collectors close to the brand or to each other. The conversations that matter most to watch enthusiasts were happening on external platforms the company had no visibility into: fragmented across independent channels, social media feeds, and comment sections, none of which were owned or shaped by The 1916 Company.
The absence of an owned community layer created a structural gap. Without ongoing interaction, the app couldn't surface what collectors were excited about, what they were considering, or what their collections actually looked like. Each transaction was isolated rather than part of a continuous relationship. The team recognized that closing that gap, bringing content, commerce, and community into one coherent digital experience, was the defining product challenge ahead of them.
<sprscript-green>Why social.plus<sprscript-green>
## Infrastructure built for the collector experience they wanted to create
The 1916 Company's vision was specific: a platform where collecting, conversation, and commerce could coexist natively, without asking users to leave the app for any part of the experience. That required in-app community infrastructure flexible enough to support multiple distinct engagement mechanics: activity feeds, structured discussion threads, user profiles, content channels, and real-time social interaction, all functioning together cohesively.
Building those capabilities in-house would have meant significant and sustained engineering investment, diverting focus from the core product and creating long-term maintenance obligations. social.plus provided the modular infrastructure to make it possible without that burden. The Social SDK gave the team the building blocks to design a community layer that felt native to the product and matched the aesthetic and culture of luxury retail, not a generic feature dropped into an existing app.
Equally important was the ownership model. With social.plus, all community activity including collector content, discussion threads, Wrist Feed logs, and user profiles would live entirely within The 1916 Company's own environment. That meant the company could develop genuine insight into what their collectors valued, what they were wearing, and what was shaping their interests over time. The community wouldn't just drive engagement; it would generate compounding intelligence that could inform everything from editorial to inventory curation.
<sprscript-green>Implementation<sprscript-green>
## A community layer built around how collectors actually behave
The centerpiece of the implementation was a new Community Tab, a dedicated hub within the redesigned app that brought all social interaction together in one place. Rather than treating community as an ancillary feature, it became the organizing principle of the new experience, the first place collectors landed when they opened the app outside of a buying intent.
The Wrist Feed became the most distinctive feature of the implementation: a daily log where collectors share what they're wearing, browse what others have on their wrists, and build a personal record of their wearing habits over time. The mechanic is deceptively simple but highly habit-forming, giving collectors a reason to open the app every day. Over time, the feed surfaces patterns, showing which pieces get the most wear, how community tastes shift with seasons and releases, and which watches in a collection tend to sit unworn.
The Discussion Feed was designed for deeper engagement: a space for conversations around product launches, industry news, collecting strategies, and market movements. The 1916 Company brought recognized voices from the watch world into those discussions directly, including journalists and reviewers with significant followings in the collector community. Their participation raised the quality of conversation and gave the community an authority that generic platforms can't replicate. Content channels organized around specific topics and major industry moments, including live coverage of events like Watches & Wonders, gave users structured entry points into the community beyond their personal networks.
Social features including post creation, likes, comments, and collection showcasing gave collectors the full toolkit to express their identity and connect with others. User profiles support both public and private collection display, letting each collector choose how much of their passion they share with the broader community.
<sprscript-green>The Results<sprscript-green>
## A platform collectors return to every day
Since launch, The 1916 Company's app has moved well beyond its transactional origins. The Community Tab has become the defining feature of the product, the reason collectors open the app when they're not shopping, and increasingly the reason they stay. The community is growing, user-generated content flows in steadily, and early feedback is actively shaping the next phase of development.
The Wrist Feed has proven to be the highest-engagement feature, driving daily opens from a user base that previously engaged with the app primarily around purchase intent. The discussion experience has attracted strong participation, with collectors engaging around purchases, trends, and major industry moments, and with the quality of expert-led conversation establishing the platform as a credible knowledge hub, not just a transactional tool.
Perhaps most significantly, the app now functions as a continuous relationship between The 1916 Company and its collectors, not a series of isolated purchase events. The community generates ongoing insight into collector behavior and interests that was simply inaccessible before. As John Shmerler, CEO of The 1916 Company, described it: "At The 1916 Company, we believe that watch collecting is about much more than what's on your wrist; it's about how our passion for watches connects us. The relaunch of our app brings that belief to life, creating a new space where knowledge is shared, habits are tracked and conversations unfold in real time."
The 1916 Company is continuing to build on this foundation. Plans include expanding social and engagement capabilities, deepening the integration between content, commerce, and community, and enhancing personalization across feeds and discussions. The long-term vision is a best-in-class digital ecosystem for collectors globally, one where the app is not just where you buy watches, but where you live your passion for them.

pages.metaDescription: Discover how Smart Fit built a digital fitness community with social.plus
pages.content:
# How Smart Fit built a digital fitness community with social.plus
Smart Fit is the largest gym network in Latin America and one of the most recognized fitness brands in the region. With thousands of locations and millions of members, the company has built its reputation on accessibility, affordability, and innovation. Its digital ecosystem plays an important role in that strategy, helping members manage workouts, follow training programs, and track their progress.
As the company continued to invest in its digital experience, the team saw an opportunity to go further. Fitness is rarely just about the exercises themselves. Motivation, encouragement, and community often determine whether someone sticks to a routine. Smart Fit wanted to capture that sense of connection inside its app and turn it into a source of motivation for members. This vision led to the launch of a new social experience powered by social.plus.
<sprscript-green>The Challenge<sprscript-green>
### Turning motivation into a daily habit
One of the biggest barriers in fitness is consistency. Even with accessible gyms and well structured workout plans, many people struggle to maintain their routine over time. Motivation can fluctuate, and without encouragement or accountability it becomes easy for workouts to slip.
Smart Fit already offered a robust mobile app where members could access workouts and track progress. However, the experience was largely individual. Members could follow training programs but had limited opportunities to interact with others who shared similar goals.
The team believed that creating a sense of community within the app could help reinforce positive habits. If members could share achievements, encourage one another, and participate in challenges together, the app could become more than a utility tool. It could become a daily source of motivation that helps members stay committed to their fitness journey.
<sprscript-green>Why social.plus<sprscript-green>
### Adding social features without rebuilding the product
When Smart Fit explored ways to introduce social interaction into the app, the product team needed a solution that would integrate smoothly with the existing platform. The goal was not simply to add messaging or comments, but to create a fully connected community experience that felt native to the product.
social.plus provided the flexibility to embed a complete social layer directly inside the Smart Fit app. Using the platform’s SDKs, the team could introduce features such as user profiles, content feeds, groups, challenges, and private messaging while maintaining a consistent user experience.
This approach allowed Smart Fit to move quickly while still delivering a rich set of engagement features. Instead of building everything from scratch, the team could focus on designing social experiences tailored to fitness and the Smart Fit community.
<sprscript-green>Implementation & Use Case<sprscript-green>
### Launching a social layer inside the Smart Fit app
The new experience was introduced through a dedicated Social tab inside the Smart Fit mobile app, available to both Android and iOS users. The feature was designed to feel like a natural extension of the existing app rather than a separate product.
Members can now create personalized profiles that include photos, short bios, and details about their fitness interests and routines. These profiles help users connect with others who share similar goals and training styles.
Within the social feed, members can publish photos, short videos, polls, and text updates related to their workouts. Other users can react and comment, turning personal milestones into shared moments of encouragement.
One of the most popular interactions revolves around members posting their completed workouts using the hashtag TáPago. These posts celebrate the effort of finishing a workout and invite others to engage and show support.
The app also allows users to join interest based groups where members discuss topics such as running, strength training, or nutrition. Challenges add another layer of engagement by encouraging members to participate in friendly competition, either individually or as teams. Private messaging enables members to connect directly, plan workouts together, and motivate each other outside the public feed.
Together, these features transform the Smart Fit app into an active community space rather than just a place to track workouts.
<sprscript-green>Results and Impact<sprscript-green>
### From fitness app to community platform
The introduction of social features has reshaped how members interact with the Smart Fit app. Instead of using the platform only when starting or finishing a workout, members now return throughout the day to engage with others in the community.
Sharing workout completions, interacting in groups, and participating in challenges creates a stronger sense of accountability and encouragement. Members celebrate their progress publicly, which reinforces positive behavior and helps build stronger habits over time.
The social environment also mirrors the camaraderie often found inside physical gyms. Members can motivate one another, exchange advice, and feel part of a broader fitness community even when they are not physically at the gym.
By embedding these experiences directly within the product, Smart Fit has expanded the role of its app from a functional fitness tool to a central hub for engagement and connection.
<sprscript-green>Future Plans<sprscript-green>
### Expanding the digital fitness community
For Smart Fit, the launch of social features is only the beginning. With social.plus as the foundation, the company now has the flexibility to continue evolving its digital community and introduce new ways for members to interact.
As the platform grows, Smart Fit plans to keep exploring how social interaction can support healthier habits, deeper engagement, and stronger connections among members.
By bringing community directly into the product experience, Smart Fit has created a digital environment where motivation, accountability, and connection work together. The result is an app that supports not only workouts, but the relationships and encouragement that make those workouts sustainable.

pages.metaDescription: Learn how Syngenta turned farmers into a community with social.plus
pages.content:
# How Syngenta turned farmers into a community with social.plus
Syngenta is one of the world’s leading agriculture companies, helping growers improve productivity, sustainability, and crop outcomes through science and technology. As digital tools become increasingly important in modern farming, Syngenta has been investing in platforms that support farmers beyond traditional inputs.
One of these tools is the Cropwise Grower App, a digital platform designed to help farmers manage their crops, access insights, and make more informed decisions in the field. In Pakistan, the platform has seen strong adoption, with more than 1 million registered farmer profiles already onboarded. Syngenta saw an opportunity to take the platform even further by creating a space where growers could learn from each other.
<sprscript-green>The Challenge<sprscript-green>
### Creating a space for growers to learn from each other
While Cropwise already provided valuable digital tools, growers still lacked an easy way to connect with one another directly within the platform. Farmers frequently face similar challenges, from pest outbreaks to irrigation strategies, and often rely on peer advice to find practical solutions. However, there was no dedicated place in the app where growers could exchange knowledge or share experiences.
This meant many farming practices remained isolated. A grower experimenting with a successful technique in one region might never reach others who could benefit from the same idea. At the same time, access to expert advice or agronomists could be limited depending on location. This left growers without immediate support when facing urgent crop issues.
Syngenta recognized that bringing farmers together could unlock significant value. By enabling growers to connect, ask questions, and share what works in their fields, Cropwise could become more than just a tool. It could become a thriving knowledge-sharing ecosystem for the farming community.
<sprscript-green>Why social.plus<sprscript-green>
### Turning Cropwise into a community-driven platform
Syngenta was looking for a solution that could seamlessly add social functionality into the existing Cropwise experience while keeping the platform simple and valuable for growers.
social.plus stood out because it allowed Syngenta to embed a full community experience directly within the app. The goal was not simply to introduce social features. The objective was to create meaningful interactions that would increase engagement while empowering growers to help each other succeed.
By integrating social.plus, Syngenta aimed to strengthen grower loyalty to the Cropwise platform while encouraging collaboration and knowledge sharing among farmers. The company also saw an opportunity to gain deeper insights into the needs and challenges of growers. Conversations within the community could help inform product development and improve the services they provide.
<sprscript-green>Implementation & Use Case<sprscript-green>
### Bringing the Cropwise grower community to life
Syngenta introduced the Community feature directly within the Cropwise Grower App in Pakistan, allowing farmers to join and participate without needing a separate platform.
Growers could create profiles and authenticate themselves within the community, giving them a recognizable identity when interacting with others. Once inside the platform, farmers can share updates from their farms, ask crop-related questions, get post recommendations, and tag specific crops to make their content more relevant.
A global feed allowed users to discover and engage with posts from farmers across the network. Growers could like, comment, share, or bookmark posts that were useful to them. These interactions helped conversations grow naturally around real farming experiences.
The platform also made it easier for growers to connect with others who shared similar interests. Farmers could follow other growers based on location or crops of interest. This helped create smaller and more relevant networks within the broader community. Crop-specific tags added another layer of discovery by helping users find discussions related to particular crops or farming practices.
By embedding the community directly into the existing app, Syngenta ensured that participation felt natural for growers who were already using Cropwise.
<sprscript-green>Results & Impact<sprscript-green>
### Rapid adoption and strong early engagement
The response from growers was immediate and overwhelmingly positive. On the very first day after launch, more than 300 unique growers joined the community and contributed over 70 posts.
The momentum continued quickly. Within the first month alone, the Cropwise Community welcomed over 15,000 growers who generated more than 6,000 posts. Engagement has remained strong, with farmers contributing an average of more than 50 posts per day.
These interactions are helping farmers share practical field insights, discover new techniques, and learn from the experiences of other growers across different regions. At the same time, the conversations provide Syngenta with valuable insights into growers’ needs, helping the company continuously improve its digital offerings.
<sprscript-green>Future Plans<sprscript-green>
### Expanding connected farming worldwide
Following the strong adoption in Pakistan, Syngenta plans to expand the Cropwise Community to additional regions across the country and eventually launch the experience in other zones around the world.
The long-term vision is to continue building a connected ecosystem where growers can collaborate, learn, and innovate together. By combining digital tools with community-driven knowledge sharing, Cropwise is evolving into a platform that supports farmers not only with data and insights but also with the shared expertise of other growers.
With social.plus powering the community experience, Syngenta is helping farmers move from isolated decision making toward a more connected and collaborative future in agriculture.

pages.metaDescription: Discover how The Ring Magazine reignited its digital presence with social.plus
pages.content:
# How The Ring Magazine reignited its digital presence with social.plus
Often referred to as The Bible of Boxing, The Ring Magazine has been a cornerstone of boxing journalism and rankings since its first issue in 1922. With over a century of legacy and a dedicated global audience, The Ring continues to set the standard for authoritative coverage of the sport. In response to a rapidly evolving digital landscape, the brand has relaunched its online presence to better serve modern boxing fans while preserving its rich history.
<sprscript-green>The Challenge<sprscript-green>
### Modernizing a legacy brand without losing its soul
With a legacy rooted in print, The Ring faced the critical challenge of adapting to a digital-first audience. Their previous website lacked modern functionality and failed to offer the level of interactivity today’s sports fans expect. Navigation was dated, the platform didn’t support meaningful user engagement, and it didn’t reflect the brand’s position as a leader in boxing media. As the team worked to bring The Ring into a new era, creating a space where fans could actively engage with the sport and each other became a top priority.
<sprscript-green>Why social.plus<sprscript-green>
### Choosing a community engine that could keep up with the fight
To bring the community element of its platform to life, The Ring selected social.plus to power the fan interaction layer of its relaunch. social.plus offered a proven, ready-to-integrate SDK that made it easy to embed dynamic, real-time community features directly into the redesigned site. The decision was driven by social.plus’s flexibility, ease of implementation, and strong focus on engagement — key criteria for building a truly interactive boxing destination.
<sprscript-green>Implementation & Use Case<sprscript-green>
### Rolling out a modern fight night forum
In close collaboration with social.plus, The Ring implemented a robust community experience called fanzone, designed specifically for boxing fans. With social.plus’s SDKs, they introduced features like threaded discussions, polls, and debate forums, enabling fans to talk about recent fights, speculate on upcoming matchups, and participate in ongoing boxing culture. The integration aligned seamlessly with the broader platform overhaul, supporting The Ring’s vision for an immersive and community-driven digital experience.
<sprscript-green>Results & Impact<sprscript-green>
### A strong foundation for engagement
With the launch of the new RingMagazine.com and its accompanying mobile app, fans can now access a range of interactive features centered around the sport they love. The community section, powered by social.plus, is one of the most distinctive upgrades — giving fans a dedicated space to discuss, vote, share opinions, and participate in trivia and other interactive elements. It’s a key part of The Ring’s commitment to not only inform but involve its audience in shaping the future of boxing conversation.
<sprscript-green>Future Plans and Conclusion<sprscript-green>
### Looking ahead to more rounds
Following the relaunch, The Ring is focused on continuing to evolve its platform and deepen engagement with fans. The community experience will remain central to this growth, with plans to explore new interactive formats and enhancements. With social.plus as a foundational partner for community features, The Ring is well-positioned to keep its legacy alive while embracing the future of sports media.

But on the app, users lacked a space to interact, share progress, and encourage one another. The company wanted to bridge this gap by integrating social features into the app, allowing users and instructors to engage in meaningful ways. # How Sawgrass built a creative community with social.plus
Sawgrass, a global leader in printer manufacturing, has long been at the forefront of creative printing solutions. In addition to producing high-quality printers, the company offers a web-based design platform that enables users to create and customize products such as t-shirts, tote bags, and drinkware. With a built-in drop-shipping model, Sawgrass empowers entrepreneurs and hobbyists to sell their personalized designs without inventory risk.
<sprscript-green>The Challenge<sprscript-green>
## Expanding Sawgrass' design platform into a community
While Sawgrass had successfully built a powerful design platform, they faced three key challenges in driving further growth. First, they wanted to increase user engagement on the software side of their business, ensuring that more customers actively used their web-based tools. Second, they aimed to evolve their platform beyond just a design tool into a thriving creative hub where users could draw inspiration from others and even sell their own custom designs. Finally, they needed an efficient way to integrate social and interactive features into their website without requiring extensive development work.
<sprscript-green>Why social.plus<sprscript-green>
## The perfect fit for seamless social integration
When searching for a solution, Sawgrass prioritized flexibility and ease of integration. social.plus provided the ideal framework to add a social layer directly into their existing platform, offering users a seamless experience where they could design, interact, and sell—all within one environment. Beyond basic social capabilities, social.plus also offered the potential to expand into chat and video features, giving Sawgrass the scalability to grow its digital ecosystem over time.
<sprscript-green>Implementation and Use Case<sprscript-green>
## Bringing social features to life with social.plus
To kick off the transformation, Sawgrass implemented social.plus within its myMate platform, introducing key social capabilities such as discussion forums, user profiles, and newsfeeds. By creating an interactive space where users could share projects, exchange ideas, and participate in design competitions, Sawgrass significantly enhanced the community experience. These features encouraged collaboration and inspiration, turning the platform into more than just a tool—it became a destination.
<sprscript-green>Results and Impact<sprscript-green>
## Driving user engagement and interaction
The early results have been promising, with steady month-over-month growth in active members on the platform. Sawgrass has seen a noticeable boost in user interactions, particularly through community-driven campaigns and design contests. The added social elements have made the platform stickier, fostering a sense of belonging among users and keeping them engaged beyond just the design process.
<sprscript-green>Future Plans<sprscript-green>
## Evolving Sawgrass' platform with AI and automation
Looking ahead, Sawgrass plans to make its online community a core pillar of its digital strategy. The company is exploring automation to streamline user onboarding and is considering AI-driven interaction features to enhance engagement further. By continuing to innovate with social.plus, Sawgrass is positioning itself as more than just a printer manufacturer—it's becoming a hub for creativity, collaboration, and commerce in the custom design space.

# How Activerse enhances fitness & wellness apps with social.plus
Activerse is a dynamic development company dedicated to building innovative health and wellness apps for personal trainers, influencers, and fitness professionals. pages.metaDescription: Discover how Talkspace is strengthening teen mental health support with social.plus
pages.content:
# How Talkspace is strengthening teen mental health support with social.plus
Talkspace, a leader in online mental health therapy, has always focused on making professional support accessible to those who need it. With a mission to provide easy, affordable, and confidential therapy, they've helped millions find relief through text, audio, and video-based therapy sessions. But beyond professional support, they recognized a growing need—users also wanted a sense of community, a space where they could connect with others facing similar struggles.
<sprscript-green>The Challenge<sprscript-green>
## Creating peer connection that's safe, supportive, and on teen terms
Teens are facing a mental health crisis. According to the CDC, over 40% of high school students report persistent feelings of sadness or hopelessness. In this emotionally complex time of life, peer connection is more than just helpful—it's vital.
But creating those connections isn't easy. Many teens struggle with social anxiety, identity formation, and fear of judgment. Much of their interaction happens online, where communication can be misread and cruelty is only a comment away. Recognizing the need for a space that reflected the way teens communicate—with honesty, emotion, and authenticity—Talkspace set out to create a peer support feature that would complement therapy, not replace it.
They needed a partner that could help them build an environment teens could trust: a space that was moderated, emotionally safe, and fully aligned with clinical goals—while still feeling natural and engaging for teens.
<sprscript-green>Why social.plus<sprscript-green>
## The right partner to build peer support within the therapy experience
Talkspace chose social.plus because it offered the flexibility to add a fully embedded social experience within the Teenspace platform—rather than bolting on a third-party forum or redirecting users to a separate app. This meant the experience could be fully customized, deeply integrated into the Talkspace therapy journey, and designed from the ground up for teens.
Crucially, social.plus also offered scalability. As Talkspace continues evolving its offering to meet the needs of different populations, social.plus provides a foundation for future expansion into features like real-time chat, short-form video, and even live interaction—ensuring Talkspace can continue to meet teens where they are.
<sprscript-green>Implementation and Use Case<sprscript-green>
## Building teenspace community: Anonymous, safe, and designed with teens
Teenspace Community was co-developed by Talkspace's clinical team alongside real teens to ensure that the experience would feel authentic, safe, and emotionally resonant. social.plus made it possible to bring this vision to life quickly and securely through its modular SDKs, allowing the Talkspace team to maintain control over both design and functionality.
The feature is now available to partners offering Talkspace teen therapy, and it's designed to function in three key ways:
Teens are prompted each day with a reflection question to encourage self-expression and emotional insight. They can respond anonymously, see how others answer, and begin to build a sense of connection and shared experience.
They can also react to and affirm each other's reflections using emojis or encouraging comments—giving and receiving support in simple, positive ways that help them feel seen and valued.
Finally, teens can choose to share their reflections directly with their therapist with a tap, providing additional insight for therapy sessions without needing to reframe their thoughts.
With continuous moderation powered by clinical experts and AI tools, Teenspace Community stays safe, supportive, and troll-free. Every participant is verified to be a real teen receiving therapy through Talkspace, ensuring authenticity across the board.
<sprscript-green>Results and Impact<sprscript-green>
## Early engagement and strong foundations for emotional growth
Though recently launched, Teenspace Community is already showing signs of meaningful impact. Teens are engaging actively, expressing themselves more freely in therapy, and building positive patterns of peer interaction.
Research supports this approach: structured peer support has been shown to reduce depression, anxiety, and loneliness, offering teens not just emotional relief, but a sense of agency and empowerment. With the combined structure of therapy and peer interaction, Talkspace is seeing deeper engagement and more holistic support for the teens in their care.
"We know that young people turn more toward their peers in this season of their lives, and that's why we are passionate about meeting young people where they are and creating a safe space for them to show up for each other," said Liz Colizza, Director of Research and Programs at Talkspace. "Peer support is increasingly recognized as an important component of supporting young people's mental health, because it allows young people to connect over shared experiences and it empowers them to provide encouragement and support for each other."
<sprscript-green>Future Plans<sprscript-green>

To address these challenges, YourJuno integrated social.plus into their app, transforming it into a hub for both education and community interaction. The onboarding process was seamless, and the team quickly adapted the platform to meet user needs, enhancing the overall experience and aligning with the app’s inclusive values.

The integration of social.plus redefined YourJuno as more than an educational platform—it became a thriving community for financial empowerment. Ulta needed a way to inspire ongoing engagement, foster loyalty, and tap into the growing demand for interactive digital experiences.
<sprscript-green>Why social.plus?<sprscript-green>

Integrated Social Layer: social.plus offered the flexibility to seamlessly embed a social community directly into Ulta’s platform, ensuring users stayed engaged without leaving the ecosystem.
Influencer and Brand Collaboration: The platform enabled Ulta to bring brand partners and influencers into the community, adding value with exclusive insights, promotions, and authentic interactions.

# How Snai elevated user engagement by integrating social.plus
## A global leader and innovator in sports betting 
Snai stands out as a leader in Italy’s dynamic sports betting industry, celebrated for their platform that seamlessly merges betting with news and live streams. They offer a wide range of betting options from sports and horse racing to virtual sports, video lottery, new slots, and a range of online and mobile gaming experiences, making them a top player in their field.
Snai has become a popular hub for gaming and sports fans in Italy with their retail network of over 1,500 betting locations across the country and their digital platform with a wide range of gaming and entertainment services. This combination caters to a diverse audience, and their commitment to keeping customers informed with the latest news through their blog demonstrates their dedication to providing an engaging and up-to-date experience for both customers and fans.
## Engaging users beyond watching games
Sports is a dynamic and fast-paced industry, but Snai wanted to find a way to engage their users beyond watching games and placing bets, ultimately increasing the time people spent using their app. But this was more than just a smart business decision that improved their app metrics. They wanted to do it in a way that was mutually beneficial, with quality content that enables meaningful connections for their users.
Snai saw an untapped potential in tipsters, the influencers of the betting world who offer predictions and insights on the outcomes of sports events or other betting opportunities. Their audience loved getting advice from tipsters, and Snai wanted to bring that experience directly to their platform, creating a seamless experience where users can engage with tipsters and make bets, all in the same app. This would stop their audiences from leaving their app and going to third-party platforms.
However, building these features from scratch would be too expensive and take too long for Snai to do in-house. They needed a ready-to-use solution that’s fast, cost-effective, customizable, and scalable. 
‍
## Powering social experiences with social.plus
‍
Snai found the powerful social features they needed to bring the influence and engagement of tipsters to their app with social.plus. Following the integration of video in their app, they were able to give tipsters the platform to host live streams directly on the Snai app, where their users go to place bets. 
This simple but smart move by Snai merged the two main activities of their users in one app, dramatically improving their user experience. Viewers joining the live streams are able to talk to other viewers during the stream and engage with the tipsters with our live comments overlay. Snai also offers an archive of stream recordings so anyone can relive the experience whenever they want, and even read the messages sent in the chat. By introducing these social features, Snai is able to keep users in their own app, where they belong.

By fueling the social side of their betting platform, Snai has harnessed the power of influencers and brought the social networking experience to their own platform. The integration of social features to their platform funnels valuable engagement from external platforms back to Snai, seriously boosting their user retention and satisfaction. 
With social.plus powering their social experiences, Snai was able to increase the time users spent on their platform outside of the scheduled game streams and create a brand new experience for both their users and tipsters. Snai is planning to add even more features soon to expand on the positive results, feedback, and revenues they achieved partnering with social.plus

pages.content:
# How Harley-Davidson revved up community engagement with social.plus
## The world’s first (and most loyal) brand community
Who doesn’t know Harley-Davidson? Founded in 1903, Harley-Davidson is the oldest and most well-known motorcycle manufacturer in the world. And unlike most vehicle manufacturers, they’ve managed something extraordinary: they’ve created a global community of riders dedicated to the Harley-Davidson lifestyle.
The Harley Owners Group (HOG) is the official club of the Harley-Davidson community. Founded in 1983, the community is made up of riders of all ages, genders, and backgrounds, and it is known for its sense of camaraderie and its love of the open road. HOG chapters are located all over the world, and they offer a variety of events and activities for members to enjoy, such as group rides, rallies, and social gatherings.
But HOG is more than just a group of people who like to ride motorcycles. It’s a lifestyle, a way of being. Harley-Davidson riders are passionate about their bikes, and they love to share their passion with others. They’re known for their sense of community and their willingness to help others.
According to Stéphane Sahakian, the brand's Community Manager in France, "Harley-Davidson is more than a product. People are buying a lifestyle, a passion. We're very aware of that and are nurturing that passion… Our primary goal is always to stay connected, engage, motivate, and keep the dream alive for our customers."
‍
<sprscript-green>The challenge<sprscript-green>
## Bringing community engagement to their app
Harley-Davidson is one of the first brand communities in the world. And their community is very active on social media, with countless groups and forums where riders can connect with each other and share their experiences. 
While the company has always focused on real-world experiences and events, they also know the importance of growing and evolving with their community members. Harley-Davidson has an official app called “H-D” where users can log the rides they go on with their bikes as they earn loyalty points by riding. This unique feature allowed riders to connect in a way that no other platform could provide.
HOG members could use the app to track their rides, but they couldn’t interact with each other on the app itself. Harley-Davidson needed a way for their people to connect and build relationships. They also wanted to find a way to connect with biking enthusiasts outside of the HOG community who want to be part of the conversation.
Harley-Davidson needed to bring this vital engagement to their own app. They were missing social features that would enable their users to post, comment, and more. But the solution they were looking for had to be easily integrated into their existing app, customizable to fit their needs, and scale along with their growing user base.
‍
<sprscript-green>The solution<sprscript-green>
## Engaging their community with social.plus
Harley-Davidson found the social features they were looking for with social.plus. Integrating ready-to-use technology into their app, they created a vibrant community space within their app where users can interact on the official Harley-Davidson platform instead of relying on third-party social networks.
The H-D app features an activity feed where users can share their photos, experiences, and achievements while engaging with others through likes and comments. Rides is a space where users can post the routes they take in a detailed and interactive map. Bikes is a space where users can post photos of their beloved Harley-Davidson motorcycles, creating valuable user-generated content (UGC). 
Harley-Davidson even found a simple and effective way to encourage organic community engagement on their app. App users can earn points for engaging with the in-app community (by creating a profile, completing challenges, and joining groups), which they can redeem on the H-D online shop. So not only do they feel appreciated for their contributions, but they’re rewarded for it as well. This rewards program perfectly illustrates Harley-Davidson’s commitment to their community, and their dedication to enriching the lives of bikers. 
<sprscript-green>The results<sprscript-green>
## Bringing decades of community magic to their app
There’s a reason why Harley-Davidson is one of the oldest and most well-known communities in the world. It’s because they care about their people, listen to their needs, and grow along with them. And as a brand known and renowned for their community, Harley-Davidson is always on the lookout for ways to keep their people engaged.
Harley-Davidson has big plans for the H-D app and digital community. They envision a space where anyone can come together to talk about their love of biking. They want their in-app community to bring together not only HOG members, but anyone who’s passionate about riding, no matter what bike they ride or how experienced they are. 
By introducing in-app social features, Harley-Davidson encourages users to share, connect, and engage like never before, with a focus on increasing member acquisition, retention, and ridership. We’re proud to power their in-app engagement, helping their global community and beyond stay connected and explore together.

## Empowering communities for a healthy life
Noom is an award-winning app that goes beyond tracking diet and exercise to offer their members science-backed methods to develop healthy habits that last a lifetime. They describe themselves as a “consumer-led digital health company that helps people live healthier, happier lives.” They empower people to take control of their health through proven behavioral science, and their success is based on their human-centric program which pairs Noom members with one of their thousands of trained coaches.
The achievements of their members depend on the strong communication they’ve established within their community, Noom Circles. Members are able to discuss their progress and any questions or issues they have with their peers and coaches whenever they need support. This communication helps them stay on track and reach their goals during their entire wellness journey.
<sprscript-green>The challenge<sprscript-green>
## Finding the flexibility to improve in-app UX
The success of Noom is closely intertwined with the success of their members. Following the meteoric rise of their community with 50 million app downloads, they soon identified three obstacles with the previous community platform they were using. By fixing these issues, Noom could improve their services to better help their members stay on track and succeed in their health and wellness goals.
1. Their established community existed outside of the Noom app, driving traffic away and making the experience less efficient for members. They needed to migrate their community to their app.
2. The off-the-shelf community platform didn’t allow Noom to customize features. This was especially problematic because they weren’t able to make posts by their coaches stand out. They needed customizable social features to make the experience their own.
And 3. They didn’t have access to valuable user data, which limited their ability to get feedback on their services and shape their strategies. They needed access to their user data to improve their user experience.
<sprscript-green>The solution<sprscript-green>
## Powerfully customizable social features with social.plus
Noom found the flexibility they need with social.plus. By integrating powerful social features into their app, Noom was able to customize their community experience to meet their exact needs. Noom Circles is an app feature that offers their members access to a variety of interest-based communities. social.plus enables users and coaches to create groups on different topics, post and comment to connect with their community, access an algorithmic feed catered to their interests, and so much more.
Noom further customized their community by giving coaches custom labels that distinguishes their posts from member posts, establishing them as leaders in Noom Circles. social.plus manual and automatic moderation tools help coaches preserve the peace in the communities they’re in charge of on a daily basis. And with social.plus powering their social features, Noom and their coaches can focus on what matters the most: The health and wellness of their members.
<sprscript-green>The result<sprscript-green>
## Growing an engaged in-app community
social.plus helped Noom bring their community to their own platform, where they belong. By removing the need for an external platform to connect with their community, Noom was able to take full control of their member’s activities, safety, and valuable usage data that gives them even more insight into their community.
The flexible customization options offered by social.plus enables Noom to create groups where coaches can build relationships with and provide support for members. Members can motivate and inspire each other to help achieve their goals through the power of community. And Noom can provide content from team, coaches, and fantastic writers to make achieving goals easy for their members.
And through their partnership with social.plus, Noom can continue adding new and different social features to keep their members engaged, because they know community is an integral part of their app.

# How Betano redefined real-time fan interaction with social.plus
## The best in the game with an eye for innovation
Betano (Kaizen Gaming) is a leading digital sports betting operator based in Greece, and is quickly becoming one of the fastest-growing tech companies in Europe with their streamlined emphasis on innovative operations. In 2022, Betano became the first sports betting company to partner with FIFA to be a European regional supporter of the World Cup. This was a monumental milestone for the company, bringing together millions of fans on their platform during the most watched football event in the world.
By focusing on emerging technologies and new opportunities, Betano manages to be at the frontline of their industry in all of the countries they operate in, including Germany, Brazil, and Canada. With a focus on not just technology but also their people, Betano has the ambitious goal of continually improving their products and services to provide the best and most trusted online gaming experience for their customers around the globe.
‍
<sprscript-green>The challenge<sprscript-green>
## Taking betting & live sports to the next level
Sports betting is a competitive industry with a very large audience. It takes a lot for a company to not only stand out, but excel at what they do. Betano took on that challenge and decided to take it to the next level. They needed to find a way to engage fans outside of the stadium and bring the action to them no matter where they are.
Betano needed a solution that could transform their customers’ experience, deliver unparalleled entertainment, and push the boundaries of online gaming through ultimate user engagement. And they needed these social features to integrate seamlessly into their platform, handle large amount of users during live events, and scale along with their growing user base.
‍
<sprscript-green>The solution<sprscript-green>
## Connecting users in real-time with social.plus
Betano found the solution they’re looking for with social.plus. Betano’s main attraction is live events, and with social.plus, they are able to add a social edge to their services, helping them level up their customer experience and user engagement. By immersing users with Live Chat capabilities during games, Betano gives their audience the ability to connect with other like-minded fans without missing out on the action.
With social.plus powering their chat, Betano transformed their viewers into active participants. This real-time engagement brought the exhilarating energy and sense of community of sports to their platform. Betano amped up the excitement even more by including Live Chat in their betting services, so users can chat while placing wages. These integrations helped solidify Betano’s position in the GameTech industry as a leader in user engagement.
<sprscript-green>The results<sprscript-green>
## Elevating engagement through exciting experiences
Sports and betting are both highly competitive and super exciting activities for everyone involved. But people need to have the right tools at their disposal to experience the excitement, especially when the experience is online.
social.plus provided Betano with just those tools, allowing their users to connect and collaborate when it matters. With the addition of Live Chat to their services, Betano is able to help fans get closer to the action, create connections that enhance their live sports or betting experience, and feel a sense of belonging in a community that means something to them. In return, Betano has seen increased engagement, better retention rates, and happy customers who become loyal brand advocates.

pages.content:
# How Promova boosted language learning engagement with social.plus
## Making language learning simple & fun
Promova is a platform that makes learning a foreign language simple, straightforward, and fun. Their app offers quick and easy lessons in 16 different languages to get users conversational fast. Their goal-oriented approach encourages users to reach daily streaks with 500+ lessons and they offer an extensive library to boost vocabulary. Users can even have private online speaking classes with tutors for a more personal learning experience.
With Promova, users have the flexibility to learn foreign languages anytime, anywhere. But what sets them apart is their community. In their own words, “We encourage learners like you from all over the world to share unique experiences.” This engaged community and support system paired with their personalized approach to language learning helps millions of users succeed and excel with Promova.
<sprscript-green>The challenge<sprscript-green>
## Building a support system through community
How many times have you started your learning journey with an app, only to stop a few weeks in? Learning a new language isn’t always easy, especially outside of the classroom. Promova saw this challenge, and wanted to create an environment around language learning that would encourage users to keep going and stay motivated at every stage.
Promova knew from the start that they needed to create a strong community of learners who could support each other. But they lacked the tools to enable their members to interact inside the app. They needed to add social features to their app to drive engagement and guarantee that their customers can make the most of their language learning experiences. But they needed a solution that would integrate quickly and seamlessly with their app and scale along with their growing community.
<sprscript-green>The solution<sprscript-green>
## Creating engagement with social.plus
Promova found the powerful social features they needed with social.plus to keep their community engaged and entertained on their app. With social.plus powering the newsfeed on their platform, they gave users a space to discuss the languages they’re learning, any challenges they face, and the successes they have along their journey. Users can join a community discussion group to find a language exchange partner to practice the material they’ve learned and improve their communication skills. social.plus enables members to like, comment, reply to comments in the newsfeed, and share posts externally to attract others to the platform. The newsfeeds are separated into topic-specific categories to further help users delve deeper into learning a new language. These newsfeed categories help keep the Promova community organized and on track, allowing users access the specific information, content, or support they need, when they need it the most. 
Promova further utilizes social.plus to enable users to chat with others inside the app. In their own words, “You can write messages and keep in touch with friends and language exchange partners through personal chats. It is an interactive space that you can use to practice live speech or share knowledge and ideas with other members of the community.” These live interactions take the Promova experience a step further, empowering their users to take control of their language learning experience and mimic the interactions of a classroom.
<sprscript-green>The results<sprscript-green>
## Digitalizing classroom interactions for success
With social.plus fueling their social features, Promova can nurture and grow their community right inside their app. And this sense of community where people can interact with other learners and instructors is crucial in Promova creating a fully immersive experience for users. These discussion groups and 1:1 chats not only boost their app engagement and retention, but helps millions of Promova users around the world succeed in their language learning journey.
The essence of learning is communities, and Promova’s partnership with social.plus enables them to digitalize the interactions of classrooms so users can take full advantage of their language-learning experience with Promova.

# How TrueID became Thailand’s #1 super app with social.plus
TrueID have transformed from Thailand’s leading media platform to the #1 super app. Discover how they use powerful social features to connect their community and level-up their services.
## Providing the complete digital experience
TrueID is the biggest entertainment platform in Thailand with 25 million monthly active users. They’re committed to providing curated media and lifestyle content for all that’s affordable and easily accessible. In their own words, “We democratize access by creating the TrueID application, a gateway to a wide variety of quality digital content including movies, TV, news, music, sports, travel, and personalized privileges.” And by 2019, they were the most downloaded app in their category.
The main source of their success is their focus on community. TrueID has partnered with social.plus to turn their platform into a super app where members can chat, engage, and interact with each other about their favorite content anytime, anywhere.
<sprscript-green>Challenge<sprscript-green>
## Bringing the stadium experience to their app
While planning for the 2018 FIFA World Cup, TrueID wanted to create an engaging experience for their members, similar to watching matches in stadiums. They wanted to give people the opportunity to cheer on their teams with other fans, no matter where they were watching the games.
They already had a community using their app, but they needed the right tools to enable them to chat about the games in real-time. They knew that the excitement of the World Cup could help them unlock the true potential of the TrueID app. But the World Cup was approaching, and they needed a quick and easy solution that could handle the all-time high traffic they were expecting on their platform. 
<sprscript-green>The solution<sprscript-green>
## Expanding chat capabilities with social.plus
TrueID had already partnered with social.plus for their internal chat tool in 2018. They reached out to ask if they could use this chat capability during their live streams both in the app as well as their TV set-top boxes. “After some amazing work by the social.plus team, we were able to give fans an exciting new experience and a feeling of community during the World Cup” Thotsaphon Maytheetanpongwanit, the Head of Communication, Community, and Commerce at TrueID explains. And with that, TrueID launched their in-stream chat feature with social.plus during the incredible atmosphere of the 2018 World Cup, enabling millions to virtually cheer alongside each other during the matches.
Seeing how engaged the fans were, TrueID realized the value of the in-stream chat feature and decided to make it a permanent part of their service, extending it to the English Premier League and other sports events, news, movies, and more. Over time, TrueID integrated even more social features into their app with social.plus to better connect their community. Users can now join groups, browse posts, share updates on their own timelines, take part in polls, follow celebrities and community contributors, make new friends, or just stay entertained between shows. Instead of relying on other social media platforms, these meaningful connections enabled by social.plus customizable social features are centralized in the TrueID app, where they belong.
<sprscript-green>The results<sprscript-green>
## Creating long-lasting excitement & engagement
With social.plus powering their in-app community, TrueID created safe spaces for their members to share and exchange ideas. According to Thotsaphon Maytheetanpongwanit, the Head of Communication, Community, and Commerce at TrueID, “These engaging social experiences drastically improved the user experience in our app. All of these new features not only enriched the experience of our members, but provided valuable customer feedback for us as well.” 
And the results speak for themselves. Since the 2018 World Cup, TrueID saw 1 million monthly community interactions, 300,000 monthly votes on polls, 15% month-on-month growth in the number of active members in their community, and a 30% average growth in their engagement rates following the integration of social.plus into their platform.
‍
